2606.09646.title: Do Video Foundation Models Understand Intuitive Physics? A Layerwise Probing Analysis
2606.09646.paper_summary: We study whether pretrained video foundation models encode intuitive-physics information in their frozen representations, and how this information varies across model families, layers, and probe types. Using frozen-feature probing on IntPhys2 and Minimal Video Pairs (MVP), we compare predictive joint-embedding models (V-JEPA), masked reconstruction models (VideoMAE), and a diffusion-based video generator (LTX-Video). V-JEPA achieves the strongest overall results across benchmarks, especially with probes that model temporal dynamics, while VideoMAE remains competitive and LTX-Video recovers weaker but non-trivial signal. Layerwise analyses show that physics-relevant information is weakest in early layers and becomes most accessible at intermediate-to-late depth, and temporal controls show that disrupting frame order substantially reduces performance, especially on MVP. Together, these results suggest that intuitive-physics knowledge emerges reliably in pretrained video representations, but its accessibility depends strongly on pretraining paradigm, representational depth, and readout mechanism.
2606.09646.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究預訓練的視訊基礎模型是否在其凍結表示中編碼直觀物理信息，以及這些信息在模型家族、層和探測類型之間的變化。<lb/>通過在 IntPhys2 和 Minimal Video Pairs (MVP) 上進行凍結特徵探測，我們比較了預測聯合嵌入模型 (V-JEPA)、遮蔽重建模型 (VideoMAE) 和基於擴散的視訊生成器 (LTX-Video)。<lb/>V-JEPA 在各基準測試中取得了最強的整體結果，特別是在建模時間動態的探測器上，而 VideoMAE 仍然具有競爭力，LTX-Video 則恢復了較弱但非平凡的信號。<lb/>逐層分析顯示，與物理相關的信息在早期層中最弱，在中到晚期深度時最易於訪問，而時間控制顯示，打亂幀順序會顯著降低性能，特別是在 MVP 上。<lb/>綜合這些結果表明，直觀物理知識在預訓練的視訊表示中可靠地出現，但其可訪問性在很大程度上依賴於預訓練範式、表示深度和讀取機制。
2606.09646.author: Samuele Punzo et.al.
2606.09646.authors: Samuele Punzo, Niccolò Caselli, Ippokratis Pantelidis, Francesco Massafra, Salvatore Lo Sardo, Mohammadreza Salehi
